detaileddescription.para: You can refer to the below stated example applications for more details on how to use v_mix driver.

sect1.title: main.c
sect1.para: Contains an example on how to use the XV_mix driver directly. This example shows the usage of the Mixer IP available in catalogue.

sect1.title: vmix_example.tcl
sect1.para: Contains a tcl file which automates the process of generating the downloadable bit & elf files from the provided example xsa file.

Example application design source files (contained within "examples" folder) are tightly coupled with the v_mix example design available in Vivado Catalogue. To run this tcl
Copy the exported example design xsa file in the "examples" directory of the driver
Launch the xsct terminal
cd into the examples directory
source the tcl file xsct%>sourcevmix_example.tcl
execute the script xsct%>vmix_example<xsa_file_name.xsa>

Script will perform following operations
Create workspace
Create HW project
Create BSP
Create Application Project
Build BSP and Application Project
After the process is complete required files will be available in bit file -> vmix_example.sdk/vmix_example_hw_platform folder elf file -> vmix_example.sdk/vmix_example_design/{Debug/Release} folder

When executed on the board the example application will perform following operations
Set Input video stream
Program VTC for video stream parameters
Program Mixer for video stream parameters
Program TPG to generate required video stream
Check for Video Lock
If Locked, run defined tests on video mixer IP
Repeat Steps 1-6 for defined video streams (1080p/60 and 4k2k/30)
Serial terminal baud rate should be set to 115200 For details, see vmix_example.tcl.
